# Light + dark theme system: first-class dual-mode tokens for the agentos module and demos

## Context
Operator audit gap (2026-07-04): "beautiful design (light and dark mode)" — the token floor (#14578, relocating into apps/agentos per the veto) and the cockpit SSOT are **dark-only**; no ticket anywhere makes light+dark a system-level contract. The only theme ticket in the tracker is #8165 (2024-era drag-item inheritance — not a system).

## The Problem
Dark-only ships half a product: the public demos (#14589/#14590) get recorded in whichever mode exists, the FM module hardcodes dark assumptions leaf-by-leaf, and retrofitting light mode later means re-auditing every view — the accretion pattern the operator just vetoed an app over.

## The Architectural Reality
The token layer is the ONE right place: mode = a token-set swap (`prefers-color-scheme` + explicit toggle), components consume semantic tokens only (never raw values — lintable), and the design SSOTs carry dual comps from birth (#14638 and the bird's-eye SSOT get this as a review criterion; the cockpit SSOT gains a light pass). Neo's theme substrate (existing theme architecture in src/) is the engine seam — this leaf binds the agentos module tokens to it, not a parallel system.

## The Fix
One PR: the dual-mode token definition on the #14578 floor (semantic token → per-mode value maps) + the mode toggle + `prefers-color-scheme` default + a no-raw-values lint check scoped to the module + dual-mode smoke render of the first shipped view.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every #14578 token resolves in both modes; toggle + system-preference default work.
- [ ] Lint rejects raw color values in module views (mechanical, not discipline).
- [ ] The demo recording profile (#14589 F7 line) gains a mode parameter.
- [ ] Design SSOTs' review checklists gain the dual-comp criterion (one-line edits, cited here).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
Re-theming portal/docs surfaces (separate initiative) · per-view light-mode polish (rides each view leaf once tokens exist).

## Related
#14578 (the floor this extends — Vega first-refusal as its owner) · #14638 + the bird's-eye SSOT (dual-comp consumers) · #14589/#14590 (recording modes) · #8165 (ancient, superseded-in-scope). Sweeps: theme search at 04:02Z — no equivalent system ticket.

## Ticket intake — `needs-narrowing` + `needs-contract-alignment` (no branch/claim)

[ARCH_ALIGNMENT] **Goal remains release-relevant; the July 4 prescription is partly superseded by merged substrate and cannot be implemented as written.**

### Intake record

- **Ticket age:** created `2026-07-04T04:05:07Z`; updated `2026-07-10T23:23:42Z`.
- **Bot stale-band:** `pre-stale` under the live 90-day stale / 14-day close workflow; neither `stale` nor `no auto close` is present. This is automation metadata only.
- **Parent/epic gate:** parent is #14560; Euclid's structured epic review is already posted at https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/14560#issuecomment-4891402239.
- **Memory prior art:** Vega session `a28f1415-37a4-4a5a-a3e4-b56adf6274ec` records the raw-CSS rejection and migration to Neo's SCSS/theme-map pipeline; Grace session `07be7801-5264-4e6c-b720-89114041a48f` records that the current dark/light `--fm-*` maps remain value-identical.
- **Duplicate / missing-close sweep:** no merged PR closes #14681, but four newer/current artifacts already satisfy or reshape major ACs.

### Current source truth

| Original surface | Live disposition |
|---|---|
| Theme pair + explicit toggle + persisted preference + system preference | **Already shipped** by #13024. `apps/agentos/neo-config.json` loads both themes; `ViewportController` reads `agentosTheme`, consumes `Neo.config.prefersDarkTheme`, and toggles/persists the active theme. |
| App-consumable `--fm-*` token layer | **Already shipped/migrated** by #14847 into `resources/scss/theme-neo-{dark,light}/apps/agentos/Viewport.scss`, with structural SCSS under `resources/scss/src/apps/agentos/**`. |
| Dual-mode design evidence | **Already demonstrated** by #14690's live light/dark design artifact. |
| Fleet CSS-in-JS removal | **Already delivered** by #14905 / #14899. |
| Light-native FM palette | **Still missing.** The complete `--fm-*` block is byte-for-value identical in the dark and light theme skins; the light file explicitly names this as #14681's scope. |
| Mechanical raw-color policy | **Still missing, but underspecified.** A naive hex/RGB ban would wrongly reject theme-skin value definitions and existing `var(--token, literal-fallback)` declarations. |
| Runtime dual-mode product proof / recording parameter | **Still missing or scattered.** This needs one exact executable evidence surface, not a generic “smoke render” claim. |

### Required body correction before implementation

The ticket currently has no T3 Contract Ledger even though it changes human-consumed theme/toggle/recording surfaces. Please narrow it to the remaining coherent lane and add a ledger that settles at least:

| Target Surface | Authority | Required behavior | Failure posture | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Light `--fm-*` skin | #14578 vocabulary + current dark skin + #14690 dual-mode artifact | Preserve semantic names; provide genuinely light-native surface/ink/state/family values without component knowledge | Missing/unresolved token fails the theme/render check; do not silently inherit the dark map | compiled-theme + computed-style contrast/render proof in both modes |
| Existing AgentOS theme selection | #13024 + current `ViewportController` | Consume unchanged; add regression coverage only if this lane touches it | Invalid persisted value must not invent a third theme | focused controller/render test |
| Structural-color guard | Neo SCSS structure/skin split + #14847 | Define the exact scanned paths and explicitly decide whether `var()` literal fallbacks are allowed; exempt theme-skin value declarations by contract | fail with file:line and token guidance, no regex false-positive policy | positive + negative guard fixtures |
| Demo/record mode | #14589/#14640 live runner surface | Name the exact mode parameter owner and how both modes are selected deterministically | unavailable theme/mode fails closed; no blind visual claim | same scenario rendered in dark + light with stable semantic log |

**Classification:** `needs-narrowing` / `needs-contract-alignment`. The remaining lane is positive ROI, but only after the body stops re-owning shipped toggle/theme-pair work and makes the lint/evidence contract executable. No branch or assignee claim from this intake.
